LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS LISA MCCORMICK AND PAUL STEELY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS SAMER NASSER AND KELLY GRAFF IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. DUE TO THE INDEFINITE AND VAGUE ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT WHICH FAIL TO IDENTIFY ANY PARTICULAR ALLEGED WRONG ATTRIBUTABLE TO ANY PARTICULAR DEFENDANT, BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE UNCERTAIN AND THAT UNCERTAINTY IS NOT CURED BY GIVING THE COMPLAINT A LIBERAL CONSTRUCTION. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, E-MAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM (L&M302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LISA MCCORMICK, PAUL STEELY, ESTATE OF ROBERT MCCORMICK'S, (DECEASED) DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.DEFENDANTS LISA MICHELLE MCCORMICK, ESTATE OF ROBERT MCCORMICK, STEELY MCCORMICK, PAUL CHARLES STEELY, AND MICHELLE MCCORMICK'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS SAMER NASSER AND KELLY GRAFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PROPERLY PLED NEGLIGENT ENTRUSTMENT BECAUSE SUCH A CLAIM REQUIRES PLEADING OF FACTS THAT THE OWNER OF THE VEHICLE ENTRUSTED THE VEHICLE TO SOMEONE KNOWN TO BE UNFIT TO DRIVE. (LINDSTROM V. HERTZ CORP. (2000) 81 CAL.APP.4TH 644, 648.) THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ONLY ALLEGES FACTS RELATED TO FAILING TO SECURE THE CAR AND ALLOWING IT TO BE STOLEN. AS A MATTER OF LAW, THIS DOES NOT SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR NEGLIGENT ENTRUSTMENT. PLAINTIFFS HAVE LEAVE TO AMEND IF THEY CAN ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH THAT NEGLIGENT ENTRUSTMENT OCCURRED VOLUNTARILY WITH KNOWLEDGE OR REASON TO KNOW OF THE DRIVER'S UNFITNESS TO DRIVE. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS)ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED ON DEFENDANTS PAUL STEELY, MICHELLE MCCORMICK, ESTATE OF ROBERT MCCORMICK, DECEASED'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT AS FOLLOWS: CCP SEC. 430.41 REQUIRES THAT COUNSEL MEET AND CONFER "IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE FILING A DEMURRER. COUNSEL'S DECLARATION INDICATES THIS DID NOT OCCUR, SO THE DEMURRER IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR UNTIL AFTER IT DOES. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED IMMEDIATELY TO MEET AND CONFER AND DEFENDANTS HAVE UNTIL SEPTEMBER 20, 2019 TO RESPOND TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-18-2019 Continued To Jan-15-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-17-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS LISA MCCORMICK, PAUL STEELY, ESTATE OF ROBERT MCCORMICK's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint is adopted as follows:

Defendants Lisa Michelle McCormick, Estate of Robert McCormick, Steely McCormick, Paul Charles Steely, and Michelle McCormick's motion to strike portions of Plaintiffs Samer Nasser and Kelly Graff's second amended complaint is granted. Paragraphs MV-2(d) and (e) on page 4 are stricken from the second amended complaint.

There are no facts alleged in the complaint to support either a cause of action of negligent entrustment or agency theory. Besides alleging all Defendants are family members, Plaintiffs fail to allege ultimate facts support an agency theory to hold Defendants vicariously liable. (See Doe v. Roman Catholic Archbishop of Los Angeles (2016) 247 Cal.App.4th 953, 969; cf. Moore v. Regents of University of California (1990) 51 Cal.3d 120, 134 fn.12 [criticizing similar secondary-liability allegations as "egregious examples of generic boilerplate"].) Furthermore, Plaintiffs state they meant to remove the negligent entrustment cause of action from the second amended complaint. (Opp. p. 3.)

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS LISA MCCORMICK, PAUL STEELY, ESTATE OF ROBERT MCCORMICK's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is adopted as follows:

Defendants Lisa Michelle McCormick, Estate of Robert McCormick, Steely McCormick, Paul Charles Steely, and Michelle McCormick's demurrer to Plaintiffs Samer Nasser and Kelly Graff's second amended complaint is overruled. Plaintiffs may plead inconsistent allegations in the alternative. (Mendoza v. Continental Sales Co. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1395, 1402.) Plaintiffs sufficiently plead a general negligence cause of action against Defendants by alleging, in the alternative, either Defendant Michelle McCormick (a.k.a. Lisa Michelle McCormick), Defendant Robert McCormick, or Defendant Steely McCormick (a.k.a. Paul Charles Steely) was the driver and/or possessor of the vehicle at the time of the alleged car accident. (See 2d. Am. Compl. p. 6-8 and Ex. 1.)

Counsel for Plaintiffs is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

There being no appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted on DEFENDANTS PAUL STEELY, MICHELLE MCCORMICK, ESTATE OF ROBERT MCCORMICK, DECEASED'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT as follows:

CCP Sec. 430.41 requires that counsel meet and confer "in person or by telephone" before filing a demurrer. Counsel's declaration indicates this did not occur, so the demurrer is ordered OFF CALENDAR until after it does. The parties are ordered immediately to meet and confer and defendants have until September 20, 2019 to respond to the second amended complaint.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-22-2019 Continued To Aug-14-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LISA MCCORMICK, PAUL STEELY, ESTATE OF ROBERT MCCORMICK's, (DECEASED) DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants Lisa Michelle McCormick, Estate of Robert McCormick, Steely McCormick, Paul Charles Steely, and Michelle McCormick's demurrer to Plaintiffs Samer Nasser and Kelly Graff's first amended complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs have not properly pled negligent entrustment because such a claim requires pleading of facts that the owner of the vehicle entrusted the vehicle to someone known to be unfit to drive. (Lindstrom v. Hertz Corp. (2000) 81 Cal.App.4th 644, 648.) The first amended complaint only alleges facts related to failing to secure the car and allowing it to be stolen. As a matter of law, this does not support a claim for negligent entrustment. Plaintiffs have leave to amend if they can allege in good faith that negligent entrustment occurred voluntarily with knowledge or reason to know of the driver's unfitness to drive.

With regard to the remainder of the first amended complaint, Plaintiffs may plead inconsistent allegations in the alternative. (Mendoza v. Continental Sales Co. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1395, 1402.) Plaintiffs' responses to interrogatories are not dispositive regarding who they may plead to be the owner and driver of the vehicle at this stage of the proceedings. (Mason v. Marriage & Family Center (1991) 228 Cal.App.3d 537, 546.) However, the Court urges Plaintiffs to concisely and clearly state which of the five named Defendants are alleged in the alternative to be the driver of the vehicle and which are alleged in the alternative to own the vehicle and to have negligently entrusted the vehicle to the driver. In addition, if Plaintiffs seek to plead a separate cause of action for negligent entrustment, that cause of action should be pled separately from any claim of negligence. (See Cal. R. Ct. 2.112.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Manny Rodriguez of the law offices of Nikolaus W. Reed (510-512-0964) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Illise M. Schulman of the law offices of Catherine A. Walsh (925-825-9500, ext. 6915) appears by telephone as counsel for defendants/moving parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTs LISA MCCORMICK and PAUL STEELY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as to both causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Samer Nasser and Kelly Graff is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend as to both causes of action. Due to the indefinite and vague allegations in the complaint which fail to identify any particular alleged wrong attributable to any particular defendant, both causes of action are uncertain and that uncertainty is not cured by giving the complaint a liberal construction. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.com (L&M302)
